Narrow strand (~200 m) with a MIX of coarse sand, pebbles and some larger rocks; water is typically very clear with rocky sides that suit casual snorkeling. The bay faces southwest/west and is exposed to prevailing NW–W winds—usually calm to moderate but can become wavy when winds pick up.
Access is via a steep, narrow road down from the hill; alternatively, you can walk from nearby Kontogialos (Pelekas) along the coast path. Limited facilities: a beachfront taverna operates seasonally and usually sets a small number of sunbeds/umbrellas. Parking is limited to a small area by the taverna plus roadside spaces uphill. No verified public showers/toilets or lifeguard.
Cautions: steep drive/walk, limited parking, uneven/rocky seabed in places and occasional wave surge when winds are onshore.

image:image.image:title: Gialos Beach: Taverna | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Gialos Beach Beach in Santorini, Greece Moderate waves and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Gialos Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Paralia Gialos is a peaceful pebbly beach on the southwest coast of Santorini, in the Akrotiri area. Sheltered within a bay near the inactive Kokkinopetra volcano, the beach offers dramatic views of cliffs, natural vegetation, and wild scenery. The sea bed is a mix of sand, small pebbles and gravel. It’s quieter than many of the more famous beaches, making it ideal for visitors who want relaxation, sun, sea and nature rather than large crowds.

image:image.image:title: Glyfa Beach: Sandy Beach · Beach Bar | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Glyfa Beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Moderate waves and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Glyfa Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Glyfa is a small, serene beach on the eastern coast of Paros, located near the villages of Drios and Aspro Chorio. The shore is a mix of golden sand and pebbles, with clear, calm aquamarine water — great for swimming, snorkeling, paddleboarding, or simply relaxing. Natural shade is provided by trees along the shore, which makes it pleasant even under strong sun. Because it’s somewhat off the main tourist routes, it attracts a more local crowd and feels peaceful.

image:image.image:title: Glyfada beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Glyfada beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Glyfada beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Glyfada is one of the island’s most popular west-coast beaches, about 15–16 km from Corfu Town. It features wide stretches of golden sand and crystal-clear turquoise waters. The bay is backed by green hills and cliffs, giving scenic sea views and occasional shade from trees or terrain. Ideal for families, couples or anyone wanting both beauty and beach-comfort.

image:image.image:title: Hanioti beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Hanioti beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Hanioti beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Hanioti (also Chaniotis) is a lively resort village whose beach stretches along the town’s seafront. The shore is a mix of sand and fine pebbles, and the sea entrance is gentle, making it suitable for families. The beach is very well integrated into the village environment, with cafés, tavernas, shops and promenades immediately behind it.
Due to its popularity, it tends to be busy in summer, especially midday.

image:image.image:title: Ipsos beach: Calm Water · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Ipsos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Ipsos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, narrow beach lining the east-coast resort of Ipsos. Shore is mainly pebbles/shingle with some sandy patches; seabed becomes sandier a few meters in. Water is typically very clear and generally calm due to the sheltered bay.
The beachfront runs directly along a busy coastal road; access is easy by car or Corfu city bus No. 7. Most sections are organized with sunbed/umbrella rentals run by cafés and tavernas across the road; there are established water-sports operators and small-boat rentals from the south-end harbor. Facilities in season include showers, with abundant food and drink options nearby.
Typical cautions: pebbly entry (water shoes helpful), frequent watercraft activity in marked zones, and traffic when crossing the road between beach and venues. Parking is mostly along the roadside; spaces can fill at peak times.

image:image.image:title: Issos beach: Blue Flag · Calm Water | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Issos beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Issos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Issos is a long, sandy stretch on Corfu’s southwestern coast, backed by impressive sand dunes and just north of the village of Agios Georgios Argyrades. The beach lies beside the protected Korission Lagoon creating a striking natural landscape. It’s ideal for swimming, wind- and kite-surfing (when winds are favorable), relaxing in the sand, and enjoying nature walks in and around the dunes.

image:image.image:title: Istro beach: Blue Flag · Calm Water | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Istro beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Istro beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Istro Beach (also known as Istro / Istron) lies on Crete’s eastern coast, about 12 km south of Agios Nikolaos on the road toward Ierapetra. It faces a gentle bay and is backed by green hills, making for a tranquil seaside setting.

image:image.image:title: Kalafatis beach: Family Beach · Organized | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Kalafatis beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalafatis beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kalafatis Beach lies on the southeastern coast of Mykonos, offering a mellow retreat away from the island’s busiest shores. The sand is predominantly fine and golden, though some areas near the water’s edge include stones or rocky patches. Past the immediate shore the seabed tends to be sandy with better footing. The water is clear and usually calm, though in breezy conditions gentle chop may develop. The beach supports both lounging and activity: sunbeds, umbrellas, showers, toilets and a beach bar are available in organized zones, and a water sports center offers windsurfing, paddleboarding and other rentals.
Access is by road, and public buses connect from Mykonos Town. Parking is available along the road and in nearby lots. Because parts of the beach are less developed, swimmers should be cautious in zones without lifeguard coverage and watch for submerged rocks near the edges.

image:image.image:title: Kalamaki beach: Family Beach · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Kalamaki beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalamaki beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, south-facing strand forming the eastern end of Laganas Bay within the National Marine Park. Predominantly fine sand with occasional small pebbles and seasonal seagrass deposits; very clear, shallow water over a sandy bottom.
Generally calm due to bay shelter, though onshore southerlies can bring light chop. Multiple streets from Kalamaki lead directly to the shore; access is easy on foot. Parking is mostly along nearby streets rather than a dedicated lot. Parts of the beach in front of hotels have rented sunbeds/umbrellas, while large stretches remain natural because of turtle protection.
Strict rules apply: beach use only from 07:00 to sunset; keep activity close to the waterline; avoid marked nests; no lights, vehicles, or motorized watersports on the nesting zone. Families favor the gentle entry, but respect the protected status and roped areas.

image:image.image:title: Kalami beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Kalami beach Pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalami beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kalami is a scenic bay on Corfu’s northeast coast, famed for its clear waters, pebbly shore, and historic charm (it’s associated with author Lawrence Durrell). The gentle coastline and lush hills behind the bay make it a pleasant spot for swimming, snorkelling, and relaxing in a less crowded setting.

image:image.image:title: Kalamitsi beach: Family Beach · Organized | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Kalamitsi beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalamitsi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kalamitsi is a tranquil, semi-circular bay beach at the southern tip of Sithonia. It features a broad, gently sloping golden sand shore and clear, calm waters, making it quite family-friendly. The beach is backed by a small village with tavernas, cafés, and guesthouses. Because of its natural protection from prevailing winds, it often offers peaceful swimming conditions.
It’s also composed of three adjoining stretches — the main central organized section, a more isolated eastern/nudist stretch, and a section in front of a campsite.

image:image.image:title: Kalantos Beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Kalantos Beach Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece Calm waters

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalantos Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kalantos is one of Naxos’s more remote southern beaches. Golden sandy shore, shallow crystal-clear water, and an undeveloped, tranquil atmosphere make it ideal for people seeking peace and nature over crowds. The beach lies in a natural bay sheltered from the northern winds. The scenery around is wild: wetlands, hills, and simple natural beauty dominate

image:image.image:title: Kalithea beach: Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Kalithea beach Beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 13 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalithea beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kalithea Beach is a well-known, lively beach fronting the village of Kallithea on the Kassandra peninsula. It features long stretches of pale sand (or soft mixed sand/pebble in parts) and is backed by hotels, shops, restaurants, cafés, and local infrastructure. It tends to be very organized and is well frequented by tourists.
Its central location makes it convenient: the beach is just a short walk from the village center, making it easy to combine beach time with local amenities.

image:image.image:title: Kalo Livadi: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Kalo Livadi Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kalo Livadi
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kalo Livadi is a long, elegant sandy beach on Mykonos’s southeastern coast, about 9-10 km from Mykonos Town. The shore is mostly white fine sand, surrounded by gently sloping terrain and low hills. The sea starts shallow, with crystal-clear waters ideal for swimming, families, and relaxed days by the sea. The beach has both lively beach clubs and quieter spots, so you can enjoy either social beach-bar vibes or more tranquil relaxation.

image:image.image:title: Kamari beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Kamari beach Sandy beach in Santorini, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 14 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kamari beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kamari is a well-known resort village on the southeastern coast of Santorini. Its beach is a long black volcanic beach / pebbly sand beach, formed by Santorini’s volcanic geology.
The shore lies under the foot of the Mesa Vouno mountain, adding dramatic backdrop. A paved promenade runs along the beach, lined with cafés, restaurants, shops, and bars.

image:image.image:title: Kapari beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Kapari beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters and 2 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kapari beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kapari is tucked behind the chapel of Agios Ioannis and feels wild and intimate. The beach has soft, fine sand with rocky edges and steep access via a dirt path or cliff descent. The sea is very clear and typically calm, ideal for swimming or snorkeling when wind is light. It is largely unorganized—there are no sunbeds, umbrellas, bars or toilets—so visitors must bring their own supplies. Access is by car or walking from Agios Ioannis; parking is roadside near the path entrance.
Because the descent is rocky and uneven, care is needed, especially wearing proper footwear. The setting is serene, and it’s often visited for its natural beauty and sunset views.

image:image.image:title: Karydi beach: Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Karydi beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Family-friendly and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Karydi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Karydi (Karidi) is a cove-style beach situated at the southern end of Vourvourou Bay. It features fine white sand and very shallow, turquoise waters, often described as resembling a calm, aquamarine pool. The beach is partially wild / natural: much of it is not organized, so you’ll find natural shade under pine trees and fewer built facilities. Because it is protected from prevailing winds, the sea is usually calm.
It’s popular, especially from mid-morning onwards, so arriving early is recommended to secure good spots.

image:image.image:title: Kastraki beach: Family Beach · Organized | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Kastraki beach Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kastraki beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long west-coast strand south of Mikri Vigla and north of Alyko/Glyfada. Predominantly fine, light sand with clear, turquoise water. Generally open to the Aegean with exposure to the northerly Meltemi; conditions range from calm to choppy depending on wind, with small waves more likely on windy days.
Access is by paved road; public bus services the area in season. Parking is mostly along the road or at venues behind the beach. Parts of the shoreline are unorganized, while several sections have managed sunbeds/umbrellas and low-key beach bars/tavernas. No widely reported lifeguard presence.
Cautions: limited natural shade; wind/wave chop during Meltemi; facilities are scattered rather than continuous.

image:image.image:title: Kathisma beach: Organized · Beach Bar | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Kathisma beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kathisma beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kathisma is one of Lefkada’s best-known and most popular beaches. Stretching for over 1 km along the west coast, it’s framed by hills and offers wide views of the deep turquoise Ionian Sea. The beach combines lively beach bars, organised sunbeds/umbrellas, and quieter ends where you can escape crowds. It has a cosmopolitan feel, especially in high season, but you can still find more relaxed spots toward the edges.

image:image.image:title: Kavouri beach: Family Beach · Organized | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Kavouri beach Sandy beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kavouri beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Kavouri Beach lies on the Vouliagmeni peninsula of the Athenian Riviera and offers a pleasant balance of ease and character. The shore is soft golden sand that continues into the sea, with a gentle slope into clear, shallow water — ideal for families. Two adjacent stretches (“Megalo Kavouri” and “Mikro Kavouri”) provide both free and organized zones. Some sectors offer umbrellas, sunbeds, showers and toilets, while others remain more relaxed. There’s modest parking along the road and nearby lots.
Typically the waters are calm, though wind-driven ripples may emerge in breezy conditions. The setting is green and tranquil, less crowded than central Athens beaches. Be aware that some parts may lack lifeguard supervision, and in off-peak zones amenities are minimal, so bring necessities just in case.

image:image.image:title: Kavourotrypes beach: Calm Water · Beach Bar | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Kavourotrypes beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kavourotrypes beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kavourotrypes is a striking cove-style beach on the Sithonia peninsula (Chalkidiki), tucked between pine woods and white rock formations. The name means “crab holes,” reflecting its rocky, sculpted coastline. The water is crystal clear, and parts of the beach are sandy or softly pebbly, blending with dramatic rocks and small coves. It’s become quite popular (so expect crowds in summer), but still retains a wild, scenic feel thanks to its natural features.

image:image.image:title: Keri beach: Family Beach · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Keri beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Keri beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Also known as Limni Keriou beach, this is a narrow, village-front shore inside Laganas Bay facing Marathonisi. The shore is mainly pebbly with some sandy patches; the seabed starts stone/pebble and turns sandy after a few meters. Water is typically very clear and, being sheltered within the bay, conditions are usually calm, though chop can increase with onshore winds and boat wake from the nearby small port.
Access is easy by road through Limni Keri. There are organized sections with rentable sunbeds and umbrellas, plus seafront tavernas. Large parking areas are available near the harbor end of the beach. Popular activities include boat trips to Marathonisi and the Keri Caves; the rocky edges and clear water suit casual snorkeling.
Cautions: mixed/pebble entry (water shoes helpful), active boat traffic from tour and rental boats, and the wider area lies within the National Marine Park (observe local rules and marked zones).

image:image.image:title: Kleftiko: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Kleftiko Beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kleftiko
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Iconic bay of white volcanic cliffs and sea caves on southwest Milos. Shoreline is mostly rocky platforms and cave inlets with a few small pockets of coarse sand/pebbles; seabed is predominantly rock/sand with very clear, turquoise water. Generally sheltered from northerly meltemi winds, but strong south winds can reduce clarity.
Access is primarily by boat tours from Adamas/Kipoi; there is also a rough hiking path (≈40–50 min from the trailhead near Agios Ioannis) that reaches the rocks above the coves. No services on site (no shade, sunbeds, toilets, or lifeguard).
Expect significant boat traffic in season; take care around anchors and when swimming through caves/arches. Suitable for snorkeling; popular for photography. There is no practical road access or dedicated parking at the site.

image:image.image:title: Kolymbithres beach: Family Beach · Organized | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Kolymbithres beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kolymbithres beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Iconic series of small sandy coves separated by smooth granite rock formations inside Naoussa Bay. Seabed is mostly sand with some rock ledges; water is very clear and typically calm/shallow due to the sheltered bay, though strong Meltemi days can bring chop. Several organized sections operate seasonally with sunbeds/umbrellas and beach bars; basic facilities (showers/toilets) are available via beach clubs/restaurants.
Access by car (short walk from small parking areas along the beach road), public bus from Naoussa, or water taxi from Naoussa port. Notable cautions: limited parking and free towel space in peak hours; uneven rock slabs between coves; crowds in summer.

image:image.image:title: Kontogialos beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Kontogialos beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kontogialos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, west-facing bay below Pelekas with predominantly fine sand and clear water. The seabed slopes gently, making entry easy, but the beach is exposed to westerly winds and can see moderate waves and shore-break on windy days.
Access is via a steep, narrow road; on arrival there are organized zones with sunbeds/umbrellas, several beachfront tavernas/bars, seasonal water-sports and basic facilities (showers/toilets). Parking is mostly paid in small lots close to the sand, with limited roadside spots in peak months.
The northern end is more developed; the southern end is quieter with rocky edges. Cautions: occasional stronger swell and currents during onshore winds; tight, busy access road; limited free parking at peak times.

image:image.image:title: Korakonisi: Snorkeling | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Korakonisi Beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Korakonisi
beach:metadata.beach:description: Korakonisi is one of the island’s most striking natural landmarks — a rocky islet connected to the coast by a narrow strip of land on Zakynthos’ rugged west side. Known for its dramatic cliffs, a natural sea arch, and deep blue waters, it’s a favorite for adventurous visitors. The rocky terrain requires some climbing and sturdy shoes, but rewards with spectacular swimming, snorkeling, and cliff-diving opportunities. This is not a sandy beach but a wild coastal experience.

image:image.image:title: Kryopigi beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Kryopigi beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Kryopigi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Kriopigi is a coastal village on the the Kassandra peninsula. Its beach is often described as a “long” beach with clear water, golden sand (or sand/pebble mix), and relatively well maintained. Kriopigi’s setting is partly backed by pine forest and greenery which gives natural shade.

image:image.image:title: Laganas beach: Family Beach · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Laganas beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Laganas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Very long, predominantly fine sand beach along the south side of Laganas Bay within the National Marine Park. Shallow entry and typically clear, but water can be less transparent near the busy resort strip and boat lanes. Generally sheltered in the gulf with mostly calm conditions; occasional chop with southerly winds. Access is easy by road through the resort; the beachfront is lined with venues and boat vendors.
The area is organized with extensive sunbed/umbrella concessions and beach bars. Boat rentals and turtle-watching trips depart from the beach; water sports operate in designated areas. Important cautions: this is a protected loggerhead turtle nesting zone—beach use is limited to daytime (approx. 07:00 to sunset), stay within the lower 5 m of shore, avoid marked nests, and keep clear of hatchlings.
Expect heavy crowds, music from bars, and frequent boat traffic in season.

image:image.image:title: Lageri beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Lageri beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters

beach:metadata.beach:name: Lageri beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Secluded sandy beach on the west side of the Santa Maria cape within Naoussa Bay. Fine sand with low dunes and scattered cedar bushes; water is typically crystal-clear and shallow near shore. Being inside the bay, waves are usually calm, though the area can be windy under the summer meltemi.
No organized facilities (no sunbeds, bars, lifeguard, or toilets). Access is via a rough/dirt road and then a short footpath; most visitors park along the narrow roadside and walk in. Also reached seasonally by small boats/water-taxis from Naoussa.
Notable cautions: limited natural shade; bring water/supplies; the southern section is commonly used by nudists; paths cross fragile dune/cedar habitat—stay on trails.

image:image.image:title: Lagomandra beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Lagomandra beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Lagomandra beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Lagomandra Beach lies on the eastern coast of Sithonia, not far from Neos Marmaras (about 6 km). The beach is known for its natural beauty: pine tree shade at the back, sand / soft shoreline, and generally calm, inviting waters. It is also officially recognized as a Blue Flag beach, due to its organization, cleanliness, and service standards.
Because part of the beach is operated by Lagomandra Beach Hotel & Suites, many of the facilities reflect resort-level services.

image:image.image:title: Lia beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Lia beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Lia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Lia Beach lies on Mykonos’s southeastern coast, beyond Kalafatis, and offers a more tranquil escape compared to the island’s busy party shores. The beach features soft, golden sand (with some rocky/pebbly patches near edges) and a gently sloping seabed. The water is crystal clear and ideal for snorkeling and diving. The beach is organized in its central sectors, providing sunbeds, umbrellas, showers, toilets, and a beach restaurant/bar. Behind the beach there is a large free parking area. Access is by road; there is no regular public bus service. Windbreaks (bamboo) are installed to provide protection in breezy conditions.
Because the beach is in a relatively remote cove, wave activity is normally low; in stronger winds mild ripples may appear. Caution: premium sunbed zones can be expensive, and in side zones the seabed may include rocks—take care entering water away from maintained parts.

image:image.image:title: Liapades beach: Organized · Beach Bar | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Liapades beach Pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Liapades beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small cove on Corfu’s west coast, about 1 km from Liapades village. The shore is mainly pebbles with some coarse sand, and the seabed deepens quickly. Water is typically very clear, with good visibility for snorkeling along the rocky sides.
The bay is somewhat sheltered but faces the west/northwest, so it can see moderate swell and chop with prevailing winds. Access is via a steep road through the village; space near the beach is limited and mainly roadside.
The beach is organized with seasonal sunbeds/umbrellas and a couple of eateries, plus small-scale rentals (pedalos/kayaks/boats) and boat trips to nearby coves like Rovinia and Chomi. Notable cautions: limited parking, slippery rounded pebbles underfoot, and frequent small-boat traffic close to swimmers.

image:image.image:title: Lissos Beach: Calm Water | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Lissos Beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Lissos Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Perched at the foot of the Lissos Gorge and facing the Libyan Sea, Lissos is a secluded, tranquil beach near Sougia in southwestern Crete. It’s reached via a scenic hike from Sougia (≈ 1–1.5 hours along the E4 path) or by boat from the village. The beach is backed by cliffs, ancient ruins (including the Asclepieion and city remains), and dense bushes, giving it a wild, contemplative ambiance.

image:image.image:title: Loggas beach: Taverna | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Loggas beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece and 2 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Loggas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Also known as “Sunset Beach”, Loggas sits beneath sheer clay/limestone cliffs at the village of Peroulades. The shore is a very narrow strip, mainly fine sand with some rocky/pebbly patches; in swell or at high tide parts can vanish. Water is typically clear but may turn cloudy when waves stir clay from the cliffs.
Exposed to westerly/northwesterly winds, it tends to be wavy rather than calm. Access is via a steep path and stairs from the clifftop; sections can be slippery or intermittently damaged by erosion. Facilities are on the rim above (tavernas/cafés); the beach itself is unorganized and has no lifeguard.
A dedicated car park sits on the clifftop by the access. Notable cautions: falling rocks under the cliff face, limited space, shorebreak in windy conditions, and steps unsuitable for those with mobility issues.

image:image.image:title: Logora beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Logora beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Logora beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, partly organized beach on Paros’ southeast coast near Piso Livadi. The shore is predominantly fine golden sand with some scattered small pebbles in places; seabed is mostly sandy. Water is typically clear and shallow close to shore, suitable for casual swimming and light snorkeling.
Exposure is to the east–southeast; conditions are generally calm but can become choppy under strong Meltemi winds.
Access is easy by road and local bus stops nearby; the beach is walkable from Piso Livadi. Facilities include sections with sunbeds/umbrellas, showers and nearby tavernas/cafés; water sports are available in season. Note that parts of the beach are managed/paid and it can get busy in peak summer.

image:image.image:title: Makris Gialos: Organized · Taverna | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Makris Gialos Mixed sand and pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Makris Gialos
beach:metadata.beach:description: Makris Gialos is a secluded yet accessible beach on the northeast coast of Zakynthos, roughly 30 km from Zakynthos Town. It features a pebbly-shingle shoreline, crystal-clear water, and impressive rock caves that are excellent for snorkeling and diving. The water gets deep quickly, so it’s better suited to confident swimmers. The scenery is wild and beautiful, making this a peaceful spot for couples or adventurers seeking some solitude.

image:image.image:title: Marathi beach: Family Beach · Organized | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Marathi beach Sandy beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Marathi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Marathi Beach lies on the Akrotiri peninsula, about 17 km east of Chania, Crete. It’s nestled in Souda Bay, making it sheltered and calm, with golden sand, shallow waters, and views of Drapanos Cape. A small pier splits two sandy coves, and it’s a popular family and local favorite thanks to its accessibility and amenities.

image:image.image:title: Marathias beach: Calm Water · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Marathias beach Pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Marathias beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small cove on the west side of Laganas Bay near Keri/Marathias. Shore is mostly pebbles and small stones; seabed is pebbly with very clear, deep, blue water—good for snorkeling. The beach sits below cliffs and pines, giving partial shelter from winds; sea is generally calm with only modest swell at times.
Access is by narrow road to tavern/roadside parking and then a short but steep footpath/descent to the shore (not ideal for limited mobility). Facilities are minimal: a few managed sunbeds/umbrellas in season; no lifeguard and usually no on-beach bars (tavernas are just above/nearby).
Notable cautions: steep access path, stony entry (water shoes recommended), water deepens quickly, limited shade and services.

image:image.image:title: Marathias beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Marathias beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Marathias beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Marathias is a long, sandy stretch on Corfu’s southwestern coast, backed by low dunes and olive groves. The sand is fine and golden, with just occasional pebbles at the water’s edge. The seabed slopes gently, so the shallow water extends well out, making it family-friendly for long wading. Water is generally calm; in typical conditions only light ripples appear, though stronger winds may stir modest chop.
The beach is well organized in its central sectors, with sunbeds, umbrellas, showers, changing booths, restrooms, and a beach bar / restaurants along the strip. At the far ends quieter zones exist with fewer amenities for those seeking more privacy. Access is by road, and parking is available close to the beach (roadside and in small lots).
Because of its length and layout, it rarely feels overpacked even in summer, though the center can be busier midday. Caution: in edge zones, the seabed may have uneven patches or rocks; swimmers should stay within supervised areas, and arriving early is wise to secure parking or shade.

image:image.image:title: Marathon beach: Family Beach · Organized | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Marathon beach Sandy beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Marathon beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Marathon Beach (along the Nea Makri / Agios Panteleimonas stretch) features a long sandy shore with a gently sloping seabed and remarkably clear shallow water; the sand is typically fine and golden, though occasional rockers or coarser grains appear near edges. The sea is usually calm, with only light chop under stronger winds. The beach is partly organized: many sections offer sunbeds, umbrellas, showers, toilets and lifeguards, while other parts remain free and more natural.
Access is by coastal road with nearby municipal parking and roadside spaces. Beach bars and tavernas line the shore. Caution: in less supervised stretches, swim carefully and watch for submerged rocks or sudden depth changes. In busy season parts may feel crowded.

image:image.image:title: Megali Petra beach: Organized · Sunbeds | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Megali Petra beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece with Organized beach and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Megali Petra beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Pebble–sand mix cove on Lefkada’s exposed west coast, named for the large shore and in-water boulders. Substrate ranges from coarse sand to fine pebbles; water is typically very clear. Open to prevailing W–NW winds, so conditions can turn wavy and the seabed deepens quickly.
Access is via a narrow, steep asphalt road from Kalamitsi; final approach includes a short footpath. Parking is limited to small paid lots near the end of the road plus some roadside spots. Seasonal rental of a few umbrellas/sunbeds may be present, but there are no permanent facilities on the beach and no lifeguard.
Cautions: limited/steep parking access, slippery rocks, sudden depth and strong shore break when swell is up. For food/drinks, options are at nearby Kavalikefta/Avali beaches.

image:image.image:title: Mesa Pigadia beach: Family Beach · Organized | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Mesa Pigadia beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Santorini, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mesa Pigadia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Mesa Pigadia is a quieter, lesser-known black-sand / volcanic pebble beach located on the southwest coast of Santorini near Akrotiri.
The beach lies in a small cove backed by cliffs, giving it a sheltered and intimate feel. It is often described as a “hidden gem” for people seeking a more peaceful beach experience away from the busy beaches of Santorini.
Mesa Pigadia is accessible via a rough / dirt road from the main road; public buses don’t come all the way — walking from nearby stops or driving is typical.

image:image.image:title: Messongi beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Messongi beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Messongi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Messonghi Beach sits on Corfu’s southeast coast beside the Messonghi River and the resort village. The shoreline is a mix of sand and shingle/pebble, with a generally gentle, shallow entry. Water is typically clear and the east-facing bay is fairly sheltered, so conditions are usually calm with small chop on breezier days.
Access is easy via the coastal road and local buses; the beach runs in front of hotels and tavernas. Parts of the shore are organized with sunbeds and umbrellas, plus seasonal water-sports and boat trip operators.
Cautions: expect pebbles at the waterline (water shoes recommended) and occasional sea urchins on rocky patches; stay within marked swimming zones due to boat traffic near the hotel piers and the river mouth.

image:image.image:title: Metamorfosi beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Metamorfosi beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Metamorfosi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Metamorfosi is a village on the Sithonia peninsula, surrounded by forest and coastline. Its beach (Metamorfosi Beach) is known for being Blue Flag and decently organized, with golden sand, clear waters, and a calm atmosphere.
The coastline here is not extremely dramatic — it’s more gentle and pleasant — making it good for relaxing, doing light activities, and staying in a village atmosphere rather than a heavy resort zone.

image:image.image:title: Mikri Vigla: Sandy Beach · Organized | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Mikri Vigla Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mikri Vigla
beach:metadata.beach:description: Mikri Vigla lies on the western coast of Naxos, about 11-18 km from Naxos Town depending on route. It offers fine white sand (combined with some dunes & headlands) and crystal-clear waters. The beach is especially popular with windsurfers and kitesurfers because of the steady winds, but also works well for families wanting a relaxed day by the sea. There are several accommodation units and tavernas nearby, so you can combine swimming with meals and longer stays.

image:image.image:title: Mizithres Beach: Sandy Beach | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Mizithres Beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mizithres Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: A breathtaking double-peaked rock formation rising from turquoise waters at the southern tip of Zakynthos, Mizithres is one of the island’s most photogenic spots. Accessible by boat from Keri or nearby beaches, it’s perfect for swimming, snorkeling, and soaking in dramatic views of cliffs and crystal-clear seas.

image:image.image:title: Molos beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Molos beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Molos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, quiet bay on Paros’ east coast with fine sand and very clear, shallow water. Generally a calm, protected shoreline; the meltemi can bring strong wind but waves are usually low. Unorganized (no fixed sunbeds/umbrellas or lifeguard); limited natural shade from tamarisk trees.
Access by paved and short dirt roads from Marmara; cars typically park along the roadside behind the beach. No Blue Flag listing. A simple taverna operates at the south end; rocky sections toward the north headland offer basic snorkeling. Popular for low-key walks and coastal trails (links to Kalogeros).
Cautions: exposure to wind, little shade/water provision, and the surrounding dunes/area fall under environmental protection—avoid disturbing vegetation and wildlife.

image:image.image:title: Mon Repos beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Mon Repos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mon Repos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small urban beach on the Kanoni side of Corfu Town, also known as the Royal Baths. The shore is mainly sand with some stones/rocky patches; the seabed is mostly sandy and the water is generally clear. Set inside Garitsa Bay, it’s sheltered from prevailing NW winds so the sea is usually calm with only light chop in stronger easterlies. Access is very easy: a signed gate on Emmanouil Theotoki Street (walkable along the Garitsa promenade from the Old Town; city bus 2A stops nearby).
The site is privately operated in summer with paid sunbeds/umbrellas and a beach café/restaurant; there are showers and toilets on site. Parking is on-street around the bay rather than a dedicated lot.
Notable cautions: space is limited in peak season; there’s a jetty/pier used for jumping and for small boats—watch for slippery surfaces and boat traffic; free unfenced areas beyond the managed section are minimal.

image:image.image:title: Monastiri beach: Family Beach · Organized | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Monastiri beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Monastiri beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Sheltered sandy cove within Paros Park (Agios Ioannis Detis peninsula), facing Naoussa Bay. Fine sand with some rocky edges near the northern side; very clear, shallow water for at least ~100 m.
The cove is well protected from the prevailing Meltemi (N winds), so conditions are typically calm with minimal wave action. Access is via an asphalt road with on-site parking, or by seasonal water taxi from Naoussa.
The beach is organized with sunbeds/umbrellas and a beach bar/restaurant; a watersports center operates in season. Popular and busy in peak months; music from the organized section and limited natural shade. Caution: rocks near the sides for snorkelers and boat traffic in the bay; arrive early for parking in high season.

image:image.image:title: Moraitika beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Moraitika beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Moraitika beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long resort beach on Corfu’s east coast, fronting the village of Moraitika. Shoreline is mostly sand with shingle/pebble patches; the seabed is sandy and shelves gently. Water is typically clear and shallow with generally calm conditions due to the sheltered east-facing aspect.
Access is easy on foot from the resort streets; buses stop on the main road and lanes lead to multiple beach entrances. The beach is organized with sunbeds and umbrellas for rent, and there are numerous beachfront tavernas, bars and cafés. Public toilets and showers are reported near organized sections. Water sports (e.g., pedalos, SUP/jet-ski/banana rides) operate in season.
Typical cautions: busy and densely set sunbed rows in high season; watch for boat and water-sports traffic near marked lanes; some pebbly/stoney patches at the entry—water shoes may help.

image:image.image:title: Mylos beach | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Mylos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mylos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, natural west-coast beach between Agios Nikitas and Kathisma with a sand-and-pebble mix and very clear Ionian water. Exposed to prevailing NW–W winds; conditions often become wavy with shore-break and a relatively steep drop-off.
No road access: reach via a steep 15–20-minute footpath from Agios Nikitas or by seasonal water-taxi from the village. There are typically no facilities on the beach and little natural shade. Use sturdy footwear for the trail and bring supplies.
Notable cautions: waves and currents on windy days, sudden depth, occasional boat traffic near the drop-off point, and no lifeguard presence.

image:image.image:title: Myrtiotissa beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Myrtiotissa beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Myrtiotissa beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Myrtiotissa (Mirtiotissa) is a hidden, secluded west-coast cove framed by steep cliffs and lush vegetation. The sand is fine and golden, though large rocks are embedded within the seabed, especially toward the edges. Water is exceptionally clear, ideal for snorkeling, and mostly calm, though wind exposure can stir mild chop. The access road is narrow and steep; visitors usually park above and descend a rugged path, or arrive by sea.
The stretch is lightly organized: a few umbrellas and loungers are available, and a small kiosk offers drinks and snacks. The beach is known to host nudist culture (officially tolerated) in parts. Caution is advised: there is no formal lifeguard service, paths down are steep, and rock hazards lie near shallower margins.

image:image.image:title: Mytakas beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Mytakas beach Sandy beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Mytakas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Mytakas (sometimes spelled “Mitakas”) is a tranquil beach on the northern coast of Milos, located within a small settlement of the same name. It’s made of two sandy bays, surrounded by gentle rocky hills, offering clear turquoise water and a relaxed atmosphere. While it has enough amenities for comfort, it remains much quieter than the big tourist beaches — a good choice for those wanting peace, sun, and sea without massive crowds.

image:image.image:title: Navagio (Shipwreck beach) | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Navagio (Shipwreck beach) Mixed sand and pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Navagio (Shipwreck beach)
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Iconic cove on the NW coast of Zakynthos, enclosed by high limestone cliffs and the wreck of MV Panagiotis. Shore is mostly white limestone pebbles/gravel with some sand; water is typically very clear and intensely blue. Fully exposed to the open Ionian, so wind and swell from the NW often build chop and surge; boat landings are frequently curtailed or cancelled in rough seas.
Access to the beach itself is only by boat when permitted; many seasons since 2018/2022 landslides have seen official bans on swimming/approach and time-limited offshore photo stops. A fenced cliff-top viewing platform is reachable by road near Anafonitria, but there are no facilities on the beach.
Notable cautions: recurrent rockfalls/landslide risk from the cliffs; periodic government/port-authority closures (including full bans on anchoring, swimming, or setting foot ashore); strong surge and falling rocks make the back of the beach hazardous even in calm weather.

image:image.image:title: Nea Fokea beach: Family Beach · Organized | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Nea Fokea beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Nea Fokea beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Nea Fokea is a coastal village on the eastern side of the Kassandra peninsula. Its beach is described as having fine sand and clear turquoise waters. It’s considered “organized” and family-friendly, with a scenic backdrop including a Byzantine tower overlooking the sea. The beach also acts as a gateway into Kassandra’s eastern coastline.
Because of its charm and moderate development, its facilities are more modest than at full resort beaches, but still comfortable for beachgoers.

image:image.image:title: Nea Moudania beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Nea Moudania beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Nea Moudania beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Nea Moudania’s beach stretches for about 2 km along the town and continues toward the beach of Dionysiou. It is mostly sandy, has shallow waters (gently sloping seabed), and is considered one of the more organized beaches in the region.
The shoreline is backed by the town’s promenade, cafés, tavernas, and services, offering a comfortable mix of beach + town amenities.

image:image.image:title: New Golden beach: Family Beach · Good for Surfing | Paros
image:image.image:caption: New Golden beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Good for surfing with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: New Golden beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, fine-sand beach on Paros’ SE coast facing Naxos. Locally called Tserdakia, it has clear, usually turquoise water and a gently sloping sandy seabed. The bay is exposed to the summer Meltemi; winds are frequent and can build chop and small waves, making it a noted wind- and wing-surf spot.
Access is straightforward by car or bus from Parikia/Naoussa; shoreline lanes run behind the beach. Parts are organized with sunbeds/umbrellas and watersports centers; nearby beachfront hotels and cafes serve the beach. Expect crowds and higher prices in peak season.
Cautions: strong winds and active windsurf corridors; occasional rocky patches/reef sections; limited natural shade."

image:image.image:title: Nidri beach: Family Beach · Organized | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Nidri beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Nidri beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Town beach on Lefkada’s sheltered east coast, opposite the islets of Madouri and Skorpios. Shoreline is a mix of coarse sand and small pebbles with generally clear, tranquil water thanks to the protected bay; short wind-chop and wakes can occur from passing boats.
Exposure is low compared with the island’s west coast, so waves are usually calm. Access is straightforward via the seafront promenade within the village; multiple paid parking areas lie a short walk behind the beach, and the waterfront is flat. Large stretches are organized by hotels and beach bars offering sunbeds and umbrellas, with unserviced spots between.
Watersports operators based in Nidri (kayak, SUP, parasailing, jet ski/boat rentals) and numerous day-cruise boats depart from the nearby port. Notable cautions: frequent boat and water-sports traffic close to shore—keep clear of craft lanes; the seabed is pebbly in places, so water shoes may help

image:image.image:title: Nikiana beach: Family Beach · Organized | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Nikiana beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Nikiana beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small east-coast village beach on Lefkada with a mixed sand-and-pebble shore and very clear, typically calm water. The southeast exposure and shelter of the mainland/channel mean waves are usually minimal except in stronger onshore breezes.
Access is easy directly off the coastal road through Nikiana; the beach sits beside the harbor/front with short walks from roadside parking. Parts of the beach are organized seasonally with sunbeds/umbrellas and a simple beach bar/cantina; otherwise it remains low-key.
Entry can be pebbly—water shoes help. Depth drops gradually and the water is often used by families; visibility is good for casual snorkeling near rocks. Pedalos/SUPs are sometimes available from local operators. Space is limited, and parking can be tight in peak season.
Watch for small-boat traffic near the pier and occasional mooring lines; avoid swimming close to the harbor approach.

image:image.image:title: Orkos beach: Sandy Beach | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Orkos beach Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece

beach:metadata.beach:name: Orkos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Orkos Beach is a chain of small sandy coves between Plaka and Mikri Vigla on Naxos’ southwest coast. The shore is predominantly fine sand with rocky outcrops separating the coves, and the water is typically very clear.
The area is exposed to the Meltemi and prevailing northerlies, so conditions are often windy with chop/waves. Access is via the main road toward Mikri Vigla and a short dirt track; parking is informal along the roadside/dirt clearings. There are no permanent facilities or lifeguards and no organized sections.
Caution: gusty winds, shorebreak and currents when the Meltemi blows, and rocks between coves.

image:image.image:title: Ornos: Family Beach · Organized | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Ornos Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Ornos
beach:metadata.beach:description: Nestled in the sheltered Ornos Bay just south of Mykonos Town, Ornos is an elegant, well-developed beach with a resort feel. The shore is gently sloping, ideal for swimming and family beach days. Ornos combines natural charm with a high level of comfort: plenty of sand, calm water, and easy proximity to restaurants, cafés, shops and accommodations.

image:image.image:title: Orthi Ammos beach: Calm Water | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Orthi Ammos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters

beach:metadata.beach:name: Orthi Ammos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Orthi Ammos lies east of the Frangokastello area on Crete’s southern coast. It is known for its expansive sand dunes, clear shallow waters, and more relaxed environment compared to its more touristic neighbor, Frangokastello. The eastern portion (Kritama) has dramatic cliffs meeting turquoise seas.

image:image.image:title: Pahia Ammos beach: Sunbeds | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Pahia Ammos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Moderate waves and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Pahia Ammos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Pachia Ammos is a broad coastal beach on the north side of the narrowest section of Crete, located about 20 km east of Agios Nikolaos and some 10 km from Ierapetra. It combines stretches of sand and pebbles, with a west section that is more sheltered by a breakwater, making the waters calmer there, while the rest can face winds and waves.

image:image.image:title: Paleochori beach: Calm Water · Organized | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Paleochori beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Paleochori beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Large south-coast bay with multicolored volcanic cliffs, clear water and a sand–pebble mix. Generally sheltered from the summer Meltemi (N) and calmer than north-coast beaches; more exposed when southerly winds blow.
Access by paved road and seasonal KTEL bus; on-site/dedicated parking is just behind the beach. Parts are organized with sunbeds, umbrellas, beach bars and tavernas along the central section; quieter, more natural stretches lie to the east/west.
Geothermal activity creates warm patches and occasional sulfur odour; hot sand and shallow-water vents can occur near rocks—wear footwear, avoid digging and heed any local signage. Good visibility for casual snorkeling; water-sports are operated in season.

image:image.image:title: Panormos beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Panormos beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Panormos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Panormos is a relaxed, elegant sandy beach on the northern side of Mykonos, about 7 km from Mykonos Town. Located in a protected bay, it features soft pale sand, shallow and clear water, and fewer crowds than many of the southern beaches. The setting combines natural beauty with upscale touches — stylish beach bars/restaurants and boutique-style accommodations mix with tranquil spots.

image:image.image:title: Panormos beach: Family Beach | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Panormos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Naxos, Greece Calm waters with Family-friendly and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Panormos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small cove at the SE tip of Naxos near Moutsouna. Shore is a mix of sand and fine pebbles; seabed mostly fine sand with clear, clean water. The bay is relatively sheltered from the prevailing northerly Meltemi, so conditions are often calm, though exposure can increase when winds turn onshore. Access is by the coastal road from Moutsouna that ends at Panermos; final approach is narrow and partly unpaved.
Parking is mainly along the roadside near the harbor. Facilities are limited: a simple taverna/beach bar sits behind the beach, but there is no evidence of formal organization with rented sunbeds/umbrellas. No lifeguard; wheelchair access is not available.
Shade is from a few palm trees; bring supplies and water. Use caution on the drive and around rocks at the cove edges.

image:image.image:title: Papafragas beach: Sandy Beach · Snorkeling | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Papafragas beach Sandy beach in Milos, Greece and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Papafragas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Tiny slot-canyon cove on Milos’ north coast near Phylakopi. The beach is a very small strip of mostly sand (some pebbles) at the end of a narrow, cliff-lined channel with exceptionally clear water and sea caves.
It is exposed to the Meltemi/north winds, so conditions range from calm on quiet days to notably wavy when it blows; the channel can funnel swell and currents. Access is on foot via a short but steep, carved path/steps cut into the rock; footing can be slippery and sections are hazardous.
Periodic safety closures/fencing may restrict descent. No facilities on site. Parking is limited at the clifftop area and along the road. Notable cautions: rockfall risk, steep descent, waves/surge in the channel.

image:image.image:title: Papikinou beach: Family Beach · Organized | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Papikinou beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Papikinou beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, narrow beach lining the east side of Adamas Bay, about 1 km from the port. Shore is mainly sand with some small pebbles and tamarisk trees offering natural shade. Water is typically clear and shallow, and—being inside the bay and sheltered from the Meltemi—usually calm with low wave energy.
Access is easy on foot, by bus, or by car along the coastal road. Parking is mostly along the roadside with limited spaces. Several cafes/tavernas sit just behind parts of the beach, and some sections operate with sunbeds and umbrellas; no verified lifeguard presence.
Cautions: stretches are close to the main road; dedicated parking is limited; organization varies by section.

image:image.image:title: Paradise beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Paradise beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Paradise beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Paradise Beach on Mykonos is world-famous for its energetic party vibe and golden sand shoreline. The sand is slightly coarse yet comfortable underfoot; near the edges there are occasional rocky bits. The sea is typically clear and inviting, with a gentle slope into bathing depth and mild waves in calm conditions. The beach is highly organized: it features dozens of sunbeds and umbrellas, multiple beach bars and restaurants, change rooms and showers, and is tightly managed by beach clubs. Water sports (jet ski, banana boat, snorkeling) operate from the beach. As afternoon turns into evening, music and DJ sets ramp up, turning the beach into a club scene that often continues late at night. Access is via road or bus from Mykonos Town; parking is available in a dedicated lot near the resort.
Because the beach is almost fully occupied by beds, free spaces are scarce and crowds are heavy especially midday and evening. Caution: music volume is high, premium beds are expensive, and some zones may impose minimum spend or club cover.

image:image.image:title: Paramonas beach: Organized · Sunbeds | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Paramonas beach Pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Paramonas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
West-facing beach on Corfu’s southwest coast near the village of Paramonas. Shoreline is mostly sand with some pebbly sections; seabed is sandy with occasional stones. Water is typically clear and shallow for a short distance, then gradually deepens. Exposure to the open Ionian means conditions are generally calm to moderate, with a tendency for wind and chop in the afternoons.
Access is by road; there is no dedicated lot—most visitors use roadside spaces near the beach.
The southern part is lightly organized with seasonal sunbeds/umbrellas and a couple of seafront tavernas; public showers and basic changing facilities are reported. No current Blue Flag listing and no reliable indication of a lifeguard. Limited natural shade; expect some pebbles at the ends and occasional boat activity offshore. Best suited for a relaxed swim and low-key snorkeling when seas are settled.

image:image.image:title: Pefkoulia beach: Organized · Beach Bar | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Pefkoulia beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece with Organized beach and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Pefkoulia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long west-coast beach between Tsoukalades and Agios Nikitas. Shore is a mix of coarse sand and small pebbles; water is typically very clear and gets deep quickly. Exposed to prevailing NW winds, so it can be wavy with a strong shore break on windy days; calmer in light winds.
Easy drive-up access directly from the main coastal road. There is an organized section with sunbed/umbrella rentals and a beach bar/taverna above the southern/central part; large stretches remain unorganized.
Parking is available in dedicated lots near the beach and along the roadside, but fills in peak season. Cautions: sudden depth, waves/swell when windy, limited natural shade away from the pines at the edges; no consistently verifiable public lifeguard or facilities beyond those of the beach bar.

image:image.image:title: Perigiali beach: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Perigiali beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Perigiali beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: It is described as one of the more untouched / natural beaches (i.e. low development) in social media / informal sources. It is a relatively quiet, natural, pebbly or mixed shore, with fishing boat activity and modest local use. It is described as one of the more untouched / natural beaches (i.e. low development) in social media / informal sources.

image:image.image:title: Perivolos beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Perivolos beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Santorini, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 12 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Perivolos beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Perivolos Beach (Παραλία Περβολός) lies on the southeastern coast of Santorini, extending between Perissa and Agios Georgios. It’s known for its black volcanic sand / pebbly-volcanic shore and stylish, lively beach club scene.
Because it is long and well organized, Perivolos offers both social, activity-rich zones (with bars, music, water sports) and more relaxed stretches away from the busiest parts.

image:image.image:title: Piperi beach: Family Beach | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Piperi beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters with Family-friendly and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Piperi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small cove on the west side of Naoussa, within easy walking distance from the village. Shoreline is mixed: sandy center with rocks/pebbles and rocky shelves at both ends; seabed becomes sand a little further out. Water is typically clear. Set inside Naoussa Bay, it is somewhat sheltered from the Meltemi; waves are usually calm but can be choppy with strong north winds.
Access is straightforward on foot from Naoussa; limited street parking above the beach with a short stair descent. Facilities vary by season and are often hotel-run nearby rather than dedicated municipal infrastructure; do not expect lifeguard or public toilets.
Notable cautions: very limited space, can be crowded in peak season; uneven/rocky entry in places and occasional stones underfoot; roadside parking is scarce.

image:image.image:title: Piso Livadi beach: Family Beach | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Piso Livadi beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Paros, Greece Moderate waves with Family-friendly and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Piso Livadi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small village beach inside the fishing harbour of Piso Livadi on Paros’ east coast. Shore is mainly sand with some pebbly patches; seabed is mostly sandy with a gentle, shallow entry and usually clear water.
The east side of Paros is exposed to the Meltemi (N–NE summer winds), so conditions range from calm to light chop; the harbour gives partial shelter but short-period waves can occur when winds pick up. Access is easy on foot from the seafront; there is a local bus stop in the village and a small municipal parking area nearby. Tavernas and cafés line the waterfront. Boat excursions to nearby islands depart from the adjacent pier.
Notable cautions: proximity to the harbour means boat traffic—stay within the swim area; limited natural shade (some trees) and the beach is small, so it can feel crowded in peak season.

image:image.image:title: Plaka Beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Plaka Beach Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Plaka Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Plaka is one of Naxos’s longest and most beautiful sandy beaches, stretching for around 4 km along the southwestern coast. With soft white/golden sand, turquoise clear water, and wide open stretches, it’s ideal for sunbathing, long walks, swimming, and relaxing. Parts of the south end feel more untouched and natural, while the more developed north end (near Maragas etc.) has beach bars and restaurants, giving visitors options between tranquility or more service.

image:image.image:title: Plakias beach: Family Beach · Organized | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Plakias beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Plakias beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Nestled on Crete’s southern coast in the Rethymno region, Plakias is a laid-back seaside village boasting a long, gently curving bay. Its beach stretches about 1.3 km and blends soft sand and fine shell matter, offering shallow waters ideal for leisurely swimming. The surrounding scenery offers hills, cliffs, and a river valley flowing into the sea, adding character beyond the shoreline.

image:image.image:title: Plathiena beach: Family Beach · Organized | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Plathiena beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Milos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Plathiena beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
North-facing cove near Plaka with a mixed sand–pebble shore and very clear, typically turquoise water. Semi-sheltered by low cliffs but exposed to northerlies (Meltemi), so it’s usually calm to moderate but can turn wavy when winds pick up.
Access is by narrow paved/part-dirt road; there is a small parking area by the beach and additional roadside parking. No scheduled bus service; reach by car, taxi, scooter or on foot from Plaka. Seasonal organization with sunbeds/umbrellas and a beach bar/food kiosk.
Shallow entry in parts; good for casual snorkeling along the rocks. Cautions: limited natural shade, facilities operate seasonally, no lifeguard, and conditions deteriorate with strong north winds.

image:image.image:title: Platis Gialos: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Platis Gialos Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Platis Gialos
beach:metadata.beach:description: Platis Gialos (Platys Gialós) is a bustling, well-developed beach on the southern side of Mykonos, about 6 km south of Mykonos Town. It features long stretches of fine golden sand and crystal-clear shallow waters, making it a very popular spot for families and tourists. The bay is semi-sheltered, so conditions are comfortable for swimming, and an inviting mix of beach clubs, restaurants, and hotels line the shore.

image:image.image:title: Pollonia beach: Family Beach · Organized | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Pollonia beach Sandy beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 9 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Pollonia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Sheltered village beach in a crescent bay on the NE of Milos with fine sand (predominantly sandy, occasional small pebbly patches) and clear, shallow water. Generally calm due to the enclosed bay; can get choppy with strong N/NE meltemi. Easy, level access directly from the waterfront road and promenade.
Seasonal organization with sunbeds/umbrellas and nearby cafés/tavernas along the shore; public showers and toilets are reported in season.
Small dedicated parking area plus on-street options near the beach. Family-friendly setting; basic snorkeling along the rocky edges. Notable cautions: limited parking at peak times; proximity to the small port and boat/traffic activity on the waterfront; exposure to wind when meltemi strengthens; no consistently verified lifeguard presence.

image:image.image:title: Porto Katsiki: Beach Bar | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Porto Katsiki Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece Moderate waves and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Katsiki
beach:metadata.beach:description: Porto Katsiki is one of the most iconic beaches on Lefkada’s western coast: dramatic white cliffs above, turquoise water below, and a wide view of the Ionian Sea. You reach it via a steep road and then descend around 80-100 steps from the cliff-top parking area. The setting is very scenic, especially early morning or late afternoon when the light is softer and crowds are lighter.

image:image.image:title: Porto Koufo beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Porto Koufo beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Koufo beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Porto Koufo is a picturesque small fishing village whose namesake beach lies in one of Greece’s deepest natural harbors. The bay is sheltered by rocky hills and cliffs, giving the sea a calm, dramatic look. The beach itself is relatively narrow and sandy, though the water becomes deep not far from the shore. It’s less “resortified” than many other Chalkidiki beaches, which helps retain a more relaxed, local feel.
Because of the narrow coastline and deeper water, it is less ideal for very young children or those who prefer wide, shallow beaches.

image:image.image:title: Porto Limnionas: Taverna | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Porto Limnionas Beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Limnionas
beach:metadata.beach:description: A dramatic rocky cove on the west coast of Zakynthos, Porto Limnionas is surrounded by towering limestone cliffs and crystal-clear waters. There’s no sandy beach — instead, natural rock platforms and carved steps give access to the sea, with spots for swimming, snorkeling, cliff-jumping, and enjoying striking views.

image:image.image:title: Porto Roma: Family Beach · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Porto Roma Mixed sand and pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Roma
beach:metadata.beach:description: Porto Roma is a charming sand-and-pebble beach on the west side of Vasilikos peninsula, known for its clear, shallow waters and relaxing atmosphere. The shore slopes gently into the water, making it a great choice for families and anyone who prefers easier beach access. The surrounding area remains relatively uncommercialised, which adds to the peaceful, traditional Greek feel.

image:image.image:title: Porto Roxa: Beach Bar | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Porto Roxa Beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Roxa
beach:metadata.beach:description: A quiet rocky cove on the island’s west coast, Porto Roxa is known for its deep blue waters and laid-back tavernas perched above the sea. Sunbeds and diving platforms are set on the rocks, making it a great spot for swimming and cliff-jumping, though it has no sandy shore. It’s ideal for those seeking a relaxed, authentic atmosphere away from the crowds.

image:image.image:title: Porto Timoni: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Porto Timoni Pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Timoni
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Porto Timoni is a hidden double-cove gem on Corfu’s northwest coast beneath the village of Afionas. Two adjacent coves, separated by a narrow spit, open into lush headlands and reveal strikingly clear water. The shores are mainly pebbles and shingle, with patches of sand or mixed substrate in the gentler parts. The seabed slopes moderately, with snorkeling opportunities near the rocky margins. The beach is fully unorganized—no infrastructure, no amenities—and access is by a steep, roughly 20–30 minute hike down well-marked but rugged paths from Afionas (or by boat from Agios Georgios). Parking exists in Afionas near the trailhead (paid or roadside).
Because of its remoteness and lack of services, waves remain calm in most conditions but wind or swell may stir surface ripples; swimmers must be cautious of uneven seabed and absence of lifeguards. Arrive early to avoid crowding, carry all your supplies, and respect the natural setting.

image:image.image:title: Porto Vromi beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Porto Vromi beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Porto Vromi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Twin small coves on Zakynthos’ west coast, each a tiny harbor with a narrow beach of white pebbles. Water is exceptionally clear and typically calm inside the coves, though swell can increase with strong westerlies and frequent boat traffic. The seabed drops off fairly quickly. Access is via a steep, winding but paved road; both sides have small dirt/rough parking areas near the water.
Facilities are minimal: seasonal sunbeds/umbrellas on limited beach space and a simple canteen/snack point; boat trips to Navagio and nearby caves depart frequently. Good for snorkeling along rocky edges.
Notable cautions: very limited beach area, constant boat movements in peak season, narrow approach road, and usually no lifeguard on duty.

image:image.image:title: Possidi beach: Family Beach · Organized | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Possidi beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 14 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Possidi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Possidi is a striking beach on the western tip of the Kassandra peninsula, known for its long sandy spit (cape) that extends into the sea. The beach often changes shape with tides and currents, giving it a dynamic character.
The cape (often called Possidi Cape or “Miti”) is a key attraction — you can walk out along the sandbar to have water on both sides.
Possidi is partly organized with facilities nearer the village, while farther stretches are more natural.

image:image.image:title: Pounta beach: Sandy Beach · Organized | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Pounta beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece with Organized beach and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Pounta beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Sandy beach on Paros’ west coast, by the Pounta (Pounda) ferry port across the narrow channel to Antiparos. Fine sand with clear, typically shallow waters. Very wind-exposed in summer (Meltemi) with steady side-shore winds; surface can be choppy and often wavy, and the area is a major kite/windsurf spot with active schools operating on the beach. Access is easy by road; buses and the Antiparos car ferry serve the adjacent port.
Large on-site parking at the port area. A small section is organized with rentable sunbeds and umbrellas; the rest is open shoreline used mostly for watersports rather than leisurely swimming.
