Notable cautions: frequent strong winds, heavy kitesurfing activity with launch/landing zones and right-of-way rules; stay clear of the ferry approach and marked watersports areas.

image:image.image:title: Preveli beach: Calm Water · Beach Bar | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Preveli beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Preveli beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Preveli sits at the mouth of the Kourtaliotiko Gorge on Crete’s southern coast, where the Megalopotamos river meets the sea and forms a lagoon before flowing into the Libyan Sea. A lush palm forest (Phoenix theophrasti) lines the banks, offering a tropical oasis feel. Behind the beach, a gorge climbs up steep terrain, giving an adventurous backdrop. The sand/pebble beach is backed by greenery and the river, making for an exotic, scenic destination.

image:image.image:title: Provatas beach: Family Beach · Organized | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Provatas beach Sandy beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Provatas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
South-coast beach with golden, fine sand and very clear, shallow water. Generally sheltered from the summer northerly (Meltemi) winds, so the sea is usually calm; however, during southerlies it can see heavier surf. Access is easy via paved road, and there is local bus service from Adamas.
Parts of the beach are organized with sunbeds/umbrellas and adjacent tavernas/beach bars; other stretches remain open. Limited natural shade. Large, dedicated parking area behind the beach.
Caution: on windy days waves can cover much of the sandy strip; observe conditions before placing belongings near the water.

image:image.image:title: Psakoudia beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Psakoudia beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Psakoudia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Psakoudia is a sandy, relatively long and wide beach on the isthmus between the Kassandra and Sithonia peninsulas (the “middle leg” connection). The beach is generally calm, gently sloping, and has been awarded Blue Flag status in many years for its cleanliness and services. It is backed by pine forest and nature, blending natural beauty and infrastructure.
Because of its location between two major peninsulas, it is sometimes chosen as a “gateway” base for exploring both sides of Halkidiki.

image:image.image:title: Psarou beach: Family Beach · Organized | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Psarou beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Psarou beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Psarou Beach sits in a graceful horseshoe bay a few kilometers from Mykonos Town, offering a refined beach experience. The sand is fine and soft, and the seabed slopes gently, giving clear and calm water well suited for relaxed swimming. It is highly organized: luxurious sunbeds and umbrellas, upscale beach bars (notably Nammos), showers, toilets, and lifeguards operate along most of the stretch. Water sports such as diving and jet skiing are available.
Parking exists but is limited and often paid, and many visitors prefer to reach the beach by bus or walking from nearby Platis Gialos. Because of its popularity, securing sunbeds may require booking, and free sandy zones are scarce early in the season. Crowds and high prices characterize peak hours.

image:image.image:title: Pyrgaki beach: Family Beach | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Pyrgaki beach Sandy beach in Naxos, Greece Moderate waves with Family-friendly and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Pyrgaki beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, quiet stretch on Naxos’ southwest coast with fine, golden sand; small inlets at the ends have pebbles. Water is typically clear and shallow near shore. The main coves are somewhat sheltered from the prevailing north (Meltemi) winds, but open sections can see moderate chop and blowing sand on windy days.
Access is by car via paved road most of the way, then a short, easy dirt section; parking is in a small free area near the beach. Facilities are minimal: no lifeguard, and services are limited to a few nearby tavernas.
Noted as suitable for families/young children; anglers use the rocky ends. Cautions: no organized rescue, limited shade, respect the protected dunes/cedar area behind the beach."

image:image.image:title: Red Beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Red Beach Sandy beach in Santorini, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Red Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Below towering red volcanic cliffs, Red Beach is a dramatic, photogenic stretch of sand and pebbles on Santorini’s southern coast. The high red rocks create a striking contrast with the deep blue of the Aegean. The sea entry is somewhat rocky and steep in places, so it’s more suitable for those who are okay with uneven terrain. Swimming is generally calm.

image:image.image:title: Roda beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Roda beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Roda beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long north-facing resort beach fronting the village of Roda. Shore is mostly sand with sections of fine shingle; water is typically clear and very shallow for a long distance, making it popular with families. The beach is organized in places with rental sunbeds and umbrellas and a watersports center; tavernas and beach bars line the promenade.
Access is easy on foot from the village and by bus/car; parking is mainly along local streets. There is seasonal accessibility infrastructure (SEATRAC) with beach track, WC, changing room and shower. Exposure to northerly winds can bring chop, but most days conditions are calm. Be mindful of designated watersports lanes and small-boat traffic just offshore.

image:image.image:title: Rovinia beach: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Rovinia beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Rovinia beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Hidden along Corfu’s west coast, near Liapades and about 20 km from Corfu Town, Rovinia is a wild, secluded cove with a striking natural ambiance. It’s surrounded by dense vegetation and cliffs, with clear turquoise water and a quiet atmosphere that appeals to nature lovers seeking a more off-beat beach experience.

image:image.image:title: Saint Nicholas beach: Family Beach · Organized | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Saint Nicholas beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Saint Nicholas beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Popular sandy bay on the Vasilikos peninsula with very clear, typically calm waters. The main shore is fine sand (with some rocky patches near the chapel headland), shelving gently—good for casual swimming and families. The beach is fully organized with sunbed/umbrella zones, an on-site beach bar and extensive water-sports operations (jet-ski, parasail, towables, SUP/canoe).
Access is by paved road; there is on-site and roadside parking, and the area is easy to reach by car/scooter. Exposure is generally limited by the bay’s orientation, so wind and wave action are usually light, though chop increases when sea-breeze picks up and when water-sports boats are active.
Notable cautions: heavy motorized water-sports traffic close to swimmers; keep clear of craft lanes and follow local signage/ranger instructions if Marine Park rules are posted (seasonal restrictions may apply).

image:image.image:title: Sani beach: Family Beach · Organized | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Sani beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 16 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Sani beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Sani Beach is part of the upscale Sani Resort complex on the Kassandra peninsula. It features a long stretch of white sand (the resort claims ~7 km of beach) with a backdrop of pine forest, luxury hotels, and a marina. The resort is built within a large private ecological reserve, giving it a mix of natural and high-end amenities.
Because it’s a managed, resort beach, many of the services are well maintained, though availability may depend on which sub-beach (Ammos, Sani Hill, Bousoulas, etc.) or hotel area you're using

image:image.image:title: Santa Maria beach: Family Beach · Organized | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Santa Maria beach Sandy beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Santa Maria beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Large sandy beach on Paros’ NE coast with clear, shallow water and views to Naxos. Mostly fine sand with occasional small pebbles near the backshore. The bay is fairly sheltered but can become choppy under strong Meltemi (N) winds.
Highly organized in sections with sunbeds/umbrellas, beach bars, and a water-sports club; quieter free sections exist at the ends. Access by paved road; a free, large dirt parking area sits directly behind the beach. Public showers and toilets are present at organized areas. Reached by car, taxi, or local bus via Naoussa.
In peak season it gets crowded and music from beach bars can be loud. Suitable for families in calm conditions; popular for snorkeling near rocky edges and for various water activities.

image:image.image:title: Sarakiniko beach: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Sarakiniko beach Beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Sarakiniko beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Sarakiniko is Milos’s iconic “moon-landscape” beach — smooth, bright white volcanic rock formations sculpted by wind and sea, surrounding a small inlet of striking turquoise water. There’s only a tiny sandy patch, most of the area is rock and cliff-edge, making it ideal for swimming in the shallow waters, exploring caves & cliffs, and doing some cliff jumping. The scenery is surreal and photogenic, especially around sunrise or late afternoon.

image:image.image:title: Sarti beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Sarti beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 12 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Sarti beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Sarti Beach is one of the longest and most famous beaches in Sithonia, stretching about 3 km along the village front. It faces the Gulf with views across to Mount Athos, which is often a visual highlight.
The beach is partially organized (sunbeds, umbrellas, beach bars) but because it is quite long, there are quieter stretches away from the main zones. It is awarded the Blue Flag in many years, reflecting water quality and services.
The shoreline is sandy (soft sand) and the water depth increases gradually, making it suitable for swimmers of different levels.

image:image.image:title: Sidari beach: Family Beach · Organized | Corfu
image:image.image:caption: Sidari beach Sandy beach in Corfu, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Sidari beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, north-facing bay in the resort of Sidari on Corfu’s northwest coast. The main beach is mostly fine sand with occasional shingle and shelves very gently into shallow water. Water is generally clear, though the sandstone gives a light yellow tint and near-shore clarity can drop when winds stir the fine sand. The bay is usually calm; conditions become choppier with strong northerlies and in the more exposed Canal d’Amour coves. Access is straightforward from the seafront with pedestrian paths and large public parking areas behind the strip.
The beach is fully organized with sunbeds/umbrellas, showers, toilets, beach bars and tavernas; seasonal lifeguard posts operate, and a wide range of water sports, boat rentals and short trips run from the shore. The very shallow gradient makes it suitable for families. Notable cautions: the adjoining Canal d’Amour has eroding sandstone ledges, narrow channels and unprotected cliff edges—do not dive or jump, and avoid the channels in rough seas where accidents have been reported.

image:image.image:title: Sougia Beach: Family Beach · Organized | Crete
image:image.image:caption: Sougia Beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Crete, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Sougia Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Sougia’s beach stretches in front of the village, forming a long (≈ 1.2 km) and wide shoreline with crystal-clear water. The setting is relaxed and scenic, backed by hills and gorges (notably Agia Irini Gorge) and serving as a quiet base for walkers, nature lovers, and those wanting to escape mass tourism.

image:image.image:title: Spathies beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Spathies beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Spathies beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Spathies is actually two small coves (a “small” and a “large” bay) located on the western coastline of Sithonia, south of Nikiti. Spathies is actually two small coves (a “small” and a “large” bay) located on the western coastline of Sithonia, south of Nikiti. The smaller cove is nearer the road (about 50 m), often more crowded. The larger cove (≈200 m) lies just beyond that, reached via a short walk; it is quieter and more peaceful. The beaches are unorganized / natural in character.

image:image.image:title: Stelida beach: Organized · Sunbeds | Naxos
image:image.image:caption: Stelida beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Naxos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 2 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Stelida beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
A quiet cove on the north side of the Stelida peninsula near Agios Prokopios. Shore is a sand-and-pebble mix with generally clear, calm blue waters in settled weather. The beach is exposed to the northerly Meltemi; conditions can become choppy and it’s best avoided when strong north winds blow.
Access is by asphalt road with a short stretch of earth road at the end; final approach is on foot.
Limited facilities: a few seasonal sunbeds/umbrellas may be set up; no reliable shade, services, or lifeguard. Parking is mainly along the access road and nearby shoulders. Take water and sun protection.

image:image.image:title: Super Paradise beach: Sandy Beach · Calm Water | Mykonos
image:image.image:caption: Super Paradise beach Sandy beach in Mykonos, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Super Paradise beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Super Paradise Beach lies in a sheltered cove on Mykonos’s southern coast, framed by Cycladic hills and granite outcrops. Its shoreline is mostly soft golden sand with some pebbly patches at the edges. The sea is very clear and inviting, and under normal conditions remains calm — though afternoon winds may stir light chop. The beach is highly organized: a major beach club hosts sunbeds, umbrellas, showers, toilets, lifeguards, a beach bar/restaurant, and DJ music events. Water sports (jet skis, parasailing, etc.) are offered by onsite operators.
Access is via road, and a private/paid parking area exists at the club. In peak summer the beach becomes crowded with a lively party atmosphere from mid-afternoon onward. Caution: premium sunbeds are expensive, some zones require minimum spend, and parts of the shore are rockier—watch your footing.

image:image.image:title: Toroni beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Toroni beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Toroni beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Toroni is a long sandy beach (over 2 km) in front of the village of Toroni, located along the western side of the Toroneos Gulf, between Tristinika and Porto Koufo. The beach blends organized and “free” zones, allowing both serviced and more natural stretches. There are ruins of a Byzantine / ancient castle (Likithos) at one end, adding historical interest.
Toroni is considered appealing to families, and the organized parts are fully serviced, while large parts of the wide beach are less restricted.

image:image.image:title: Tripiti beach: Calm Water | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Tripiti beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters

beach:metadata.beach:name: Tripiti beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small, secluded cove on Paros’ south coast between Drios and Aliki. Shore is a mix of sand with pebbles and rocks; water is typically very clear. Sheltered from the prevailing northerly Meltemi winds, so conditions are commonly calm to light-moderate.
Access is via a short unpaved track and a brief walk from the main road; there is no dedicated parking and space is limited along the roadside.
The beach is unorganized—no sunbeds, umbrellas, bars, or toilets—so bring water and supplies. Some tamarisk trees offer limited natural shade. No lifeguard. Take care on rocks and in occasional swell, and note the lack of services nearby (closest options are at Glyfa/Drios).

image:image.image:title: Tsigrado beach: Calm Water · Snorkeling | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Tsigrado beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Milos, Greece Calm waters and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Tsigrado beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Tsigrado is a strikingly beautiful, small cove on the southern coast of Milos, near Firiplaka. The beach is tucked between steep volcanic cliffs, with turquoise waters, natural caves, and dramatic rock formations. The final approach involves climbing down from a cliff via ladders and ropes, which makes it adventurous and photogenic — not always the easiest of access, but many say worth the effort.

image:image.image:title: Tsilivi beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Tsilivi beach Sandy beach in Zakynthos, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Tsilivi beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long, mainly sandy bay on Zakynthos’ northeast coast with pockets of small pebbles. Water is typically very clear and shallow for a long distance, making it popular with families. The east-facing bay is generally calm; light onshore breezes can bring a bit of chop by midday. Access is easy by road through the resort of Tsilivi/Planos, with ample nearby parking and multiple organized sections run by hotels and beach clubs.
Facilities include extensive sunbed/umbrella rentals, beach bars and tavernas, and a wide range of water-sports operators (jet ski, parasailing, pedalos). Regular boat trips also depart from the beach/nearby small port to the Blue Caves and Navagio.
Cautions: can be very busy in peak season with dense rows of sunbeds; swimmers should watch for water-sports traffic close to shore.

image:image.image:title: Vani beach | Milos
image:image.image:caption: Vani beach Pebble beach in Milos, Greece and 3 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vani beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small remote cove below Cape Vani on Milos’ NW coast, adjacent to the historic manganese mine. Shoreline is mostly small pebbles with some coarse grit; seabed similar. Waters are typically very clear but the site is exposed to the Aegean, so with prevailing N–NW Meltemi winds it often has noticeable swell and chop; calmer on light-wind or southerly days.
Access is primarily by boat tours that circuit the island; reaching it by land involves rough unpaved 4x4 tracks and a short hike, and many visitors view it from the cape rather than swim. There are no facilities or shade, and the mine ruins/cliffs back the cove. Not ideal for families or long swims; best for scenery and photos.

image:image.image:title: Varkiza beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Varkiza beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Varkiza beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Varkiza Beach lies along the Athenian Riviera and blends relaxed charm with full service. Much of the shore is sandy with scattered pebbles, and the seabed slopes gently, giving clear, shallow water for a good stretch. The beach is split into organized zones—sunbeds, umbrellas, beach bars and cafés under management—and more open public stretches.
Access is easy from Poseidonos Avenue or the coastal road, and public transport reaches close by. On-site parking (including valet) is offered. The beach sees waves mostly small to moderate, though winds (especially from the sea) may stir light chop. Be prepared for crowds on summer weekends and extra cost for prime locations. Follow lifeguard rules and respect paid vs free zones.

image:image.image:title: Vasiliki beach: Family Beach · Organized | Lefkada
image:image.image:caption: Vasiliki beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Lefkada, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vasiliki beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Long bay on south Lefkada with a mainly pebbly shoreline and some sandy sections (more sand toward Ponti). Water is typically clear with shallow entry in places. Mornings are usually calm/light onshore; by early afternoon a strong cross-shore thermal wind develops, drawing windsurfers and creating flat to choppy conditions rather than true surf.
Access is easy from the village seafront promenade, with several organized sections offering sunbeds/umbrellas, beach bars and tavernas. Watersports centers operate for windsurfing, SUP and kayak. Parking is mostly along the coastal road; a free lot exists at the Ponti end of the bay.
Cautions: afternoons get busy with boards and boat activity—swimmers should stay near shore or marked zones; frequent water-taxi/boats depart from the port; wind can be strong later in the day; dedicated public toilets/showers are limited and mainly found in venues behind the beach.

image:image.image:title: Vlychada Beach: Sandy Beach · Organized | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: Vlychada Beach Sandy beach in Santorini, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 4 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vlychada Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Vlychada Beach lies on the southern coast of Santorini, about 13-14 km from Fira. The beach is known for its dramatic volcanic geology: towering white cliffs sculpted by wind and sea, a long shoreline of gray sand mixed with pebbles, and deep, clear waters. Its lunar-like landscape gives it a peaceful, almost surreal vibe, making it ideal for those seeking something scenic and less crowded than the more tourist-heavy beaches. The water close to shore is relatively shallow to begin with, but deepens quickly.

image:image.image:title: Voula beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Voula beach Sandy beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 11 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Voula beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Voula Beach is one of the most popular seaside escapes along the Athenian Riviera, just a short ride from the city. The coast here is sandy and welcoming, with shallow, crystal-clear water that makes it easy to wade in and safe for families with children. The atmosphere feels lively yet relaxed, with organized sections offering rows of sunbeds and umbrellas, beach bars and cafés where you can grab a drink or snack, and lifeguards keeping an eye on swimmers.
Getting here is simple by car, tram or bus, and the large on-site parking makes it hassle-free if you drive. While most of the shore is managed and comfortable, you can also find quieter corners at the edges for those who prefer to spread a towel on the sand. On summer weekends it fills up quickly, but that is part of its charm—Voula is a place where locals and visitors mingle, enjoying the easy beach life so close to Athens.

image:image.image:title: Vouliagmeni beach: Blue Flag · Family Beach | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Vouliagmeni beach Sandy beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Blue Flag certified, Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 8 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vouliagmeni beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Set on the sheltered Saronic Gulf in the suburb of Vouliagmeni, this 650-m, ticketed municipal beach is known for very clear, regularly monitored waters. The shore is predominantly sandy with easy entry. Thanks to the protective bay, seas are generally calm; light chop can occur with southerly winds.
Access is straightforward via Poseidonos Avenue and OASA buses, and there is an on-site free parking area. Facilities include lifeguards, showers, toilets, changing areas, accessible ramps and amenities for disabled swimmers, sunbeds/umbrellas, and café/refreshments.
Note: paid admission; follow lifeguard guidance and swim within marked zones.

image:image.image:title: Vouliagmeni Lake: Family Beach · Organized | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Vouliagmeni Lake Beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Lifeguard on duty, Family-friendly and 10 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vouliagmeni Lake
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Lake Vouliagmeni is a rare thermal-brackish lake framed by rocky cliffs and pine woodland, about 20 km south of Athens. Rather than a sandy beach, swimmers access the lake from decks and platforms and a short pier—there is no broad sandy shoreline. The water is remarkably clear, gently warm year-round (circa 22-29 °C), and quite calm given its sheltered basin. The facility is fully managed with sunbeds, umbrellas, changing rooms, showers, café/restaurant, lifeguards, and accessible walkways. Parking is available for visitors.
Because of its therapeutic waters and enclosed nature, there are no high waves, but wind gusts over the lake may produce light surface ripples. As a protected natural monument (Natura 2000), diving into underwater caves is prohibited.

image:image.image:title: Vourvourou beach: Family Beach · Organized | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Vourvourou beach Sandy beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters with Organized beach, Family-friendly and 12 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vourvourou beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Vourvourou is a cluster of beaches and coastal stretches around the small village of the same name on the eastern side of Sithonia. The coastline faces a bay dotted with about nine small islands (notably Diaporos) which give parts of the sea a lagoon-style feel.
The beaches are generally sandy or soft sand, with shallow, clear waters, and the setting is more relaxed and natural than many resort beaches. The combination of islands, caves, shallow bays and calm seas makes it especially appealing for swimming, snorkeling, and boat exploration.
Because the area is less intensely developed than big resort zones, some stretches have basic infrastructure, while others remain more wild or minimally serviced.

image:image.image:title: Voutakos Beach: Calm Water | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Voutakos Beach Beach in Paros, Greece Calm waters

beach:metadata.beach:name: Voutakos Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: Voutakos is a peaceful, semi-secluded beach on the southwestern side of Paros, located between Aliki and Pounda. The shore is a mix of soft golden sand and small pebbles, and the sea is generally calm — often calmer than many more exposed beaches on the island, especially when the northern winds pick up. Ideal for those wanting a relaxing beach day in a more natural, low-crowd setting.

image:image.image:title: Vravrona beach: Calm Water · Taverna | Attica
image:image.image:caption: Vravrona beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Attica, Greece Calm waters and 5 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Vravrona beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Vravrona Beach lies just east of the archaeological site of Brauron in eastern Attica, and feels like a quiet local escape. The sand is coarse to medium with patches of pebbles near the shore, and the seabed includes rocky stretches so swimmers should watch their footing. The water is fairly clear, though not as pristine as more sheltered bays, and under calm weather the surf is mild. Access is via local roads off the coastal route from Markopoulo or Artemida. Parking is informal — roadside or small lots by the beach. Few formal services or organized zones are evident, though some taverna and café options lie nearby. There is little evidence of lifeguards or structured safety services, so swimming should be cautious.
Portions of the shoreline nearer to hotel or private areas may be more maintained, but much remains natural and low-key. Be especially cautious if waves or wind pick up, and avoid submerged rocks when entering the water

image:image.image:title: White Beach | Santorini
image:image.image:caption: White Beach Beach in Santorini, Greece Moderate waves

beach:metadata.beach:name: White Beach
beach:metadata.beach:description: A small secluded cove on the southwestern coast near Akrotiri, White Beach is framed by tall, dramatic white cliffs that give the place its name. Though the cliffs are pale, the shore consists of pebbles and grey/black volcanic sand, creating a striking contrast with the turquoise sea. The waters are clear and shallow close to shore, perfect for swimming and snorkeling. Because it’s secluded, it's more peaceful than many of the more popular Santorini beaches.

image:image.image:title: Xifara beach: Snorkeling | Paros
image:image.image:caption: Xifara beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Paros, Greece Moderate waves and 1 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Xifara beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small, quiet cove on the road from Naoussa to Santa Maria (≈3–4 km from Naoussa). Shore is a sand–pebble mix with very clear, shallow waters. It lies on the NE side within Naoussa Bay and is exposed to the summer Meltemi (north) winds, so the sea is usually calm to mildly choppy but can get more agitated on windy days. Access is via paved road; parking is informal along the roadside.
The beach is largely non-organized (occasional private umbrellas may appear), with no reliable on-site services. Good for a quick swim and basic snorkeling along the rocky patches. Notable cautions: limited shade, no lifeguard or toilets, and wind/waves when Meltemi blows.

image:image.image:title: Xigia Sulfur beach: Organized · Taverna | Zakynthos
image:image.image:caption: Xigia Sulfur beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Zakynthos, Greece Moderate waves with Organized beach and 6 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Xigia Sulfur beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Small northeast-facing cove on Zakynthos’ east/northeast coast known for natural sulfur springs that turn the clear water a milky-turquoise. Shore is a mix of smooth white pebbles and coarse sand; water becomes deep quickly with rocky patches. Generally sheltered but exposed to northerly/easterly winds—conditions vary from calm to a light chop, with occasional stronger wave action.
Access is via steep stairs from the roadside above; space on the beach is limited and it fills quickly in high season. Facilities typically include a handful of managed sunbeds/umbrellas and a taverna above the beach that serves food to the shore; very limited dedicated/paid parking by the descent plus additional roadside spots.
Notable cautions: distinct sulfur smell, cold springs, slippery rocks underfoot, sudden depth, crowding, and no lifeguard.

image:image.image:title: Zografou beach: Calm Water · Taverna | Chalkidiki
image:image.image:caption: Zografou beach Mixed sand and pebble beach in Chalkidiki, Greece Calm waters and 7 amenities

beach:metadata.beach:name: Zografou beach
beach:metadata.beach:description:
Zografou is a secluded cove-style beach lying south of Vourvourou, between Vourvourou and Armenistis. It is part of a camping / bungalow area (Camp Porto Elea) and remains less crowded than many main beaches.
The shore is a mix of sand and pebbles / small rocks, with deeper water not far from the coast. It is surrounded by rock formations and lush vegetation in the hinterland. Because it is somewhat hidden / less developed, it attracts people who prefer quieter, more natural beach settings.
One user review notes: “almost no sand, mostly small rocks … a lot of rocks in the water” and warns of sea urchins near ends.
